items.description: Narrow vertical band of yellow-brown discoloration running the full height of the right edge, approximately 8-12 mm wide.
items.scholarly_note: Typical age- or handling-related staining on the fore-edge of a bound vellum manuscript.

items.description: Small irregular loss at the extreme top-right corner with a small protruding white fiber or parchment fragment.
items.scholarly_note: Minor mechanical damage consistent with repeated opening or shelving wear.

items.description: Frayed and slightly darkened lower right corner with localized abrasion and a small dark spot.
items.scholarly_note: Common corner wear in a 13th-century codex from use and binding pressure.

items.description: Isolated tiny dark speck approximately 1 mm in diameter.
items.scholarly_note: Likely flyspeck or environmental deposit; no association with text or deliberate mark.

items.description: Three to five additional minute dark specks of sub-millimeter size distributed across the otherwise blank area.
items.scholarly_note: Environmental contaminants with no palaeographic significance.

items.description: Slight shadow and minor irregularity along the left edge consistent with binding.
items.scholarly_note: Inner margin shows expected compression and slight offset from the binding structure.

damage_notes: Yellow-brown fore-edge discoloration along right margin, full height
Frayed and abraded bottom-right corner with localized darkening
No evidence of deliberate scratching, scoring, or surface abrasion beyond natural wear
summary: Folio appears almost entirely blank with no visible text, marginalia, doodles, pen trials, or corrections. Only minor age-related physical damage and scattered environmental specks are present, concentrated on the right edge and corners. No ruling lines, ink marks, or intentional interventions are observable in the image.
